themes.exampleQuote: Sahil has been very kind & helpful with accepting custom orders whenever I come with my friends.
The employee there, Sahil, provided outstanding service. He was so attentive and did everything perfectly.
The staff were extremely rude and dismissive, which made the atmosphere feel unwelcoming.
Tried Indian food for the first time and I loved it. Excellent service!
Reva Modern Indian Cuisine is hands down our number one restaurant in Pittsburgh.

notable_quote: Reva first opened in June 2024 and I have been a regular customer ever since — no other Indian restaurant can match the level.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the food's depth and a specific server named Sahil by name, but a pattern of staff-level arrogance complaints — one incident involving a customer being asked if it was their 'first time in the US' — sits unresolved inside an otherwise strong five-star majority.
quarter_narrative: Reva opened less than two years ago and has already pulled 527 Google reviews with 80 percent at five stars — a clip that belongs to a restaurant twice its age. No Pittsburgh TikTok creators have indexed it yet, which means one of East Liberty's most-talked-about Indian kitchens is generating real word-of-mouth without a single frame of short-form video to show for it. In the Pittsburgh Asian Kitchens family, Mola leads this issue on creator collaboration fit; Reva matches that signal on paper but has no creators in the room to test it against. The Instagram account has gone dark — 71 days without a post, zero reels in 30 days — which leaves a 37.85 percent engagement rate sitting on a dormant feed, an unusually high number for an account doing nothing. The rank holds at number one in Pittsburgh Indian restaurants this issue, but the gap between Reva's street-level reputation and its digital visibility is wide enough that a single active creator cycle could move it into the Icons conversation before year's end.
tldr_read: Dominant Google presence and a conversion path with no gaps, but the Instagram has been silent for over two months and TikTok hasn't found it yet — Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Indian Restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The review engine is already running faster than almost any comparable Indian restaurant in the city; what's missing is any visual momentum layer that captures it, and that gap is the ceiling keeping this out of Icons.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram before creators do
playbook.action: The account has been silent for 71 days with no reels posted this month — one weekly reel of the kitchen or plating reactivates a 37.85% engagement rate that's waiting.

playbook.headline: Get a creator into Reva's dining room
playbook.action: No Pittsburgh TikToks are indexed for this restaurant yet — one food creator visit could be the first video the algorithm surfaces for 'Indian food Pittsburgh.'

playbook.headline: Surface the Sahil story in the bio
playbook.action: The Instagram has no bio at all — three reviewers named a specific staff member by name, and that specificity is a hook the profile page currently wastes.
